scope: Migrate sworn's Go status.json carriers (open_deferrals, verification.violations) up to the slice-status-v1 object shape so the autonomous loop reads real coach-produced status without dying on the D6 unmarshal, round-trips it loss-free and byte-stably, reconciles need_ids->covers_needs, and adds the inconclusive result enum + acknowledged_by write-back tolerance.

reachability.evidence: manual-smoke-step (AC-08), non-destructive against the live fired repo (~/projects/fired, git clean before and after — Rule 11 restore verified). The board oracle and `sworn run`'s RunSlice share the exact state.Read code path that crashed the original dogfood run ('RunSlice: read status: cannot unmarshal object into Go struct field Status.open_deferrals of type string'). (1) BEFORE/AFTER on REAL object-form data: `sworn board --release 2026-06-16-critical-journey-resolutions` on slice S05-engine-owned-real-dollars (4 object-form open_deferrals): OLD installed binary reports 'S05-engine-owned-real-dollars — unknown' (state.Read swallowed the D6 unmarshal failure); NEW binary reports 'S05-engine-owned-real-dollars — verified [verifier]' (object form parsed, true state recovered). (2) DIRECT state.Read (the exact failing line) on the AC-08 target slice S01-networth-hierarchy-remap AND on the real 4-deferral S05 status.json: both read without error; S05's why/tracking parse into named fields and the unknown coach keys (acknowledged_by, acknowledged_at, field, id) are preserved in Extra — confirming AC-01/AC-03/AC-10 on live data (real deferrals carry acknowledged_by and NO acknowledgement). (3) `sworn board` full output greps to 0 occurrences of 'cannot unmarshal object'. Artefacts: scratchpad/ac08-reachability.txt, scratchpad/ac08-direct-read.txt. NOTE: the AC-08 target release's board.json carries a SEPARATE, out-of-scope sibling bug (BoardRecord.release as an object, not open_deferrals) flagged in design.md Risk #4; it does not affect the slice status-read path under test.
reachability.smoke_step: cd ~/projects/fired && <new-sworn> board --release 2026-06-16-critical-journey-resolutions  # S05 reads 'verified' where the old binary read 'unknown'

delivered.item: AC-01: state.Read unmarshals fired's real object-form open_deferrals {id,description,why,tracking,acknowledged_by} and violations {gate,description,evidence} without error.
delivered.evidence: internal/state/state.go Deferral/Violation UnmarshalJSON; test TestRead_ObjectFormDeferralsAndViolations_NoUnmarshalError; live: ac08-direct-read.txt (S05 read OK)

delivered.item: AC-02: read->write->read->write preserves every field (incl. unknown keys) and is byte-stable across writes.
delivered.evidence: map-based MarshalJSON (sorted keys) in state.go; test TestRoundTrip_PreservesFieldsAndIsByteStable

delivered.item: AC-03: OpenDeferrals []Deferral and Verification.Violations []Violation are typed structs with an Extra overflow map preserving unknown keys.
delivered.evidence: internal/state/state.go Deferral/Violation; test TestRead_TypedStructsPreserveUnknownKeys; live Extra keys [acknowledged_at acknowledged_by field id] preserved on S05

delivered.item: AC-04: display-only consumers read via Status.DeferralStrings()/Verification.ViolationStrings() projections; oracle SliceState.Violations stays []string; unrelated report types untouched.
delivered.evidence: state.go projection helpers; oracle.go:236/259, ledger.go:157, implement.go:187, proof_record.go:74 repointed; router.go/route.go/validate_blocked.go unchanged (consume []string)

delivered.item: AC-05: CheckBoundaryMocks/isDeclared take []state.Deferral and match on Item+Why only; enforcement stays at least as strict; undeclared boundary mock still fails closed.
AC-06: state.Status NeedIDs->CoversNeeds (json tag need_ids->covers_needs); writers spec_record.go and task.go updated.
delivered.evidence: internal/state/state.go CoversNeeds; internal/implement/spec_record.go; cmd/sworn/task.go; test TestWriteSpecRecord_ParsesSpecAndWritesJSON (covers_needs read)

delivered.item: AC-07: slice-status-v1 verification.result enum includes 'inconclusive'; merge gate keys on state not result so it can never pass.
delivered.evidence: internal/baton/schemas/slice-status-v1.json; test TestSchema_InconclusiveResultValidates; merge.go:105 gates on state.Verified

delivered.item: AC-08: the exact RunSlice status-read path succeeds on the live fired repo; the previously-unreadable real object slice now reads its true state.
AC-09: go build ./... and go test ./... pass (full suite, 39 packages ok, 0 FAIL).
AC-10: open_deferrals required-set relaxed to anyOf[acknowledgement | acknowledged_by]; write-back of a real acknowledged_by-only deferral succeeds; a neither-key deferral fails closed.
delivered.evidence: internal/baton/schemas/slice-status-v1.json anyOf; tests TestWrite_AcknowledgedByOnlyDeferral_NoError, TestSchema_OpenDeferralAcknowledgementAnyOf (acknowledged_by/acknowledgement pass; neither + missing-tracking fail closed)

delivered.item: Backward-compat: legacy string-form open_deferrals/violations still parse (one-way upgrade to object on write-back), so existing on-disk boards keep working.
delivered.evidence: state.go Deferral/Violation UnmarshalJSON string branch; internal/board oracle_test legacy fixtures pass

divergence: AC-10 write-path enforcement reading. The live state.Write calls the legacy structural baton.Validate (which never evaluates open_deferrals), not the draft-2020-12 baton.ValidateSchema. The wholesale Validate->ValidateSchema rewire is explicitly deferred in internal/baton/validate_schema.go ('lands as a deliberate follow-up so a single commit does not flip every record's enforcement at once') and, measured here, would break writes outside S01's AC set (cmd/sworn/task.go writes covers_needs ['N/A-task-mode'] which violates the schema's ^N-\d+$ pattern; internal/mcp defer_slice writes a deferral with no tracking). So AC-10 is satisfied as: schema anyOf (load-bearing) + a write-path positive test (real acknowledged_by shape writes clean under the legacy validator) + a schema-level negative test (baton.ValidateSchema rejects a neither-key deferral). The wholesale state.Write -> ValidateSchema rewire remains the named keystone step-1b follow-up, filed as #39. This is the most faithful in-scope reading and avoids an out-of-AC architecturally-significant change.

Touchpoints subset: of the 16 planned touchpoints, internal/verify/validate_blocked.go (len-only on Violations), internal/router/router.go and cmd/sworn/route.go (consume the oracle SliceState.Violations which stays []string by D2) needed no change. The design predicted validate_blocked.go would be 'len-only, likely no change'. Touching fewer files than planned, not more; no out-of-track file touched.

Legacy string-form read tolerance was added to the Deferral/Violation unmarshalers (not explicitly in the AC set) because the migration would otherwise make sworn unable to read its own previously-written string-form status.json files — a regression for every existing board, contrary to the operational-readiness goal. The design's 'no flatten-to-string read tolerance' constraint is about write-back (which still always emits object form); upgrading a legacy string to object on read does not conflict with it.
